context.project_summary: A 3D space exploration game built with Unity 6 using the Universal Render Pipeline (URP). Players fly a spaceship through procedurally generated star systems, collect resources, and upgrade their ship. Uses C# MonoBehaviour components, ScriptableObject data assets, and an event-driven architecture via ScriptableObject event channels.

directory_layout.Assets/ScriptableObjects/: SO data assets (ship configs, item definitions, event channels)

rules.content: Never use C# constructors on MonoBehaviour classes. Use Awake() for self-initialization and Start() for cross-component references.
rules.rationale: Unity controls MonoBehaviour instantiation. Constructors are called before Unity initializes the object.

rules.content: Cache GetComponent<>() calls in Awake(). Never call GetComponent<>() in Update(), FixedUpdate(), or LateUpdate().
rules.rationale: GetComponent() is a slow lookup. Calling it every frame causes GC pressure and performance degradation.

rules.content: Never allocate heap memory in Update(), FixedUpdate(), or LateUpdate(). No string concatenation, LINQ queries, or 'new' expressions in hot paths.
rules.rationale: Heap allocations in the game loop cause garbage collection spikes and frame rate hitches.

rules.content: Use [SerializeField] private instead of public fields for inspector-exposed values. Use public properties for read access.
rules.rationale: Public fields expose implementation details and bypass encapsulation.

rules.content: Do not store runtime-mutable session state in ScriptableObjects. They persist between Play Mode runs in the Unity Editor.
rules.rationale: State stored in ScriptableObjects during Play Mode persists after stopping, causing hard-to-debug test pollution in the editor.

rules.content: Store Coroutine return values when you might need to stop them: private Coroutine _myRoutine = StartCoroutine(MyRoutine());
Use gameObject.CompareTag() instead of gameObject.tag == "Player" string comparison.
rules.rationale: CompareTag() avoids string allocation and is faster than == comparison.

rules.content: Since Domain Reload is disabled, static fields and events must be explicitly reset in [RuntimeInitializeOnLoadMethod(RuntimeInitializeLoadType.SubsystemRegistration)].
rules.rationale: Without Domain Reload, static state persists between Play Mode runs. Failure to reset causes subtle test pollution.

rules.content: Always release AsyncOperationHandle references when done with Addressables. Store handles to release them in OnDestroy().
rules.rationale: Unreleased handles cause memory leaks that accumulate over long play sessions.
